title: The File of Fatima - Episode 1: Illuminations Before Starting
speaker: Sheikh Abdul-Halim al-Ghizzi

content.text: Video Overview: Episode 1

In this opening episode, Sheikh al-Ghizzi introduces the **File of Fatima** as a collection of historical documents and truths that have been systematically concealed or "registered against an unknown person" for centuries. He argues that the "Perfect Religion" can only be represented by the Infallible Imams and that scholars have only relative authority. Using the Quranic "Law of Concurrence," he explains that those who justify past oppressions are partners in them. The episode moves into primary documentation from Sunni "Sahihs" and historical texts, revealing Imam Ali's view of the early Caliphs as "liars and treacherous," and documenting the specific threat and physical assault—including the breaking of Lady Fatima's rib and the miscarriage of her son, Mohsin.
Segment 1: Introduction and Intentions

Sheikh al-Ghizzi opens with a spiritual salutation to Lady Fatima and her descendants. He explains that his intention is to condense the most essential points of this file so it can be translated into major languages for the global Shi'a youth. He clarifies that his goal is not to provoke, but to present Truths and Documents from the depths of history and Hadith books—both Shi'a and Sunni—concerning the tragedy and oppression faced by the daughter of the Prophet.

Peace be upon Fatima the chosen one and al-Murtada, her husband and the Infallibles from her children. Peace be upon the Fatimids in every time and place her Shi'a and her lovers, and the mercy of Allah and His blessings. The File of Fatima - Episode 1: Illuminations before starting. There are points I wished to point out before I deal with the papers of this file.

**The First Point:** I had wished that the episodes of this program would be long, so that I could search in it every small and large thing related to this subject, near or far. This was my intention at the beginning, in this manner and fashion. But it occurred to my mind that this program should be translated into the most important languages spoken by the majority of the Shi'a of the Ahl al-Bayt in the world. We will try to translate it into Persian, Turkish, Urdu, and English. The generations of the Shi'a youth of the Ahl al-Bayt who live in this era where the crowding of life, with all its effects of civilization and the noise of technology and the problems that are renewed every day, makes many of them extremely distant from such truths and such meanings.

Therefore, I will focus my talk in these episodes on the most important matters and on the requests that are the foundation of this file. So that the program comes in line with its new plan, to be a support for all those who speak these different languages. I will try to be brief, but it is a brevity that does not neglect or diminish the important demands around which the papers of this file revolve.

**The Second Point:** The talk in these episodes is not a provocation to anyone, nor out of a love for excitement or noise. It is a statement of **Truths**. These truths are present in the books of the Shi'a of Fatima, and in the books of others. The File of Fatima: Papers from the depths of the books of Hadith and books of History. The File of Fatima: Truths and Documents in the depths of history upon which the voice of the oppressors or the forgery of the pens of historians and traditionists—from the "Worshippers of the Bride" and those who ate the crumbs of the tables of the Sultans—has prevailed.

The heinousness of the crime was recorded against "an unknown person" (didda majhul). This is how history judged the oppression of Fatima: either denial, or skepticism, or forgery, or distortion. And in the best of cases, it is recorded against an unknown person so that the File of Fatima disappears into the world of oblivion and is placed in the "Archive of Negligence" where the dust of lack of loyalty accumulates.
Muhammad (blessings of Allah be upon him and his family), when the Seal of the Prophets stood in his noble mosque and preached to his companions, saying:
Allah has cursed whoever prevents the hired worker from his wage. And I am your hired worker.
Say: I do not ask you for any reward for it except love for the kinsfolk. [Quran 42:23]

And I ask myself, before I ask anyone else: Who is the person in this world who is closest to the Messenger of Allah? Regarding the kinship connection, in the simplest linguistic meanings, in the time of Muhammad and after his departure from this world? Is there anyone more closely related in kinship and more intense in flesh and connection than Fatima? Fatima is the daughter of Muhammad. Muhammad left the world and has no one in this world—no father, no mother, no son—except Fatima.
Segment 2: The Perfect Religion and the Necessity of Truth

Sheikh al-Ghizzi emphasizes that "Perfect Religion" can only be represented by the "Perfect Ones" (Infallible Imams). He clarifies that while scholars have authority, it is relative and based on their proximity to the Ahl al-Bayt. He frames the opening of "The File of Fatima" as a necessity based on the Quranic principle of not concealing the truth, drawing a parallel to historical Israelites who rejected truths despite knowing them.
The talk here is about Fatima about the oppression of Fatima about the tragedy of the daughter of Muhammad (blessings of Allah be upon him and his family).

**The Third Point:** My conviction in this is not a proof (hujjah) over anyone, and I do not want to impose my opinion on anyone. Nor do I represent any particular entity. **Shi'ism does not represent anyone except the Ahl al-Bayt.**
This day I have perfected for you your religion. [Quran 5:3]

A **Perfect Religion**—the perfect religion is not represented except by the Perfect. And the Perfect is the Infallible only. Shi'ism does not represent anyone except Ali, and those on the path of Ali. A Perfect Religion cannot be represented by the Deficient, no matter how much they have reached in virtue, righteousness, knowledge, or jurisprudence. The authority of the people of knowledge and jurisprudence is only an accidental and relative authority, to the extent of their proximity to the Ahl al-Bayt. As stated in the noble Tawqi':
And as for the occurring events, refer regarding them to the narrators of our Hadiths, for they are my proof over you, and I am the Proof of Allah over them.

Their authority here is a relative authority. The Complete and Absolute Authority belongs to Muhammad and the Family of Muhammad. Therefore, these are our convictions that we speak about, and the matter is left to the minds, insights, and hearts.

**Question: Why am I opening this file?** Why the talk within the lines of the decision of this file? The answer comes from the two categories: The Book and the Progeny. In the Holy Book we go to Surah al-Baqarah, verse 91. The address is to the Children of Israel. The Jews who were living in Medina in the time of Muhammad:

And when it is said to them: Believe in what Allah has sent down, they say: We believe in what was sent down to us. And they disbelieve in what came after it, while it is the truth confirming that which is with them.
Then the Quran asks them a question that reveals the historical crime:
Say: Why then did you kill the Prophets of Allah before, if you were [indeed] believers?

The Jews in the time of Muhammad did not kill the Prophets. Those who killed the Prophets were their ancestors centuries before. So why does the Quran address the Jews of Medina as if they killed the Prophets? Because they were **pleased with the actions of their ancestors**. They sought to justify and hide the crimes of the past. This is the logic of the Quran: the one who is pleased with a crime is a partner in it. When we speak about the File of Fatima, we are dealing with a crime that changed the course of this Ummah. To remain silent about it is to be pleased with the deviations that followed.
Segment 3: The Law of Concurrence and Historical Responsibility

Sheikh al-Ghizzi elaborates on the Quranic logic of holding people responsible for the crimes of their ancestors if they justify them. He applies this "Law of Concurrence" to Islamic history, arguing that those who justify the oppression of Lady Fatima today are partners in that crime. He asserts that the "File of Fatima" is opened to break the "culture of silence" that has protected oppressors for centuries.

The Quran asks: "Why then did you kill the Prophets of Allah before?" Did the Jews in the time of the Prophet kill the Prophets? No, but the Quran holds them responsible. Why? Because of the **Law of Concurrence**. If you are pleased with an action, you are a partner in it. If you seek to justify the crimes of your ancestors, the crime is transferred to your record. This is the danger of "theology without truth." When we try to "whitewash" history, we become accomplices in the oppression.

The Jews of Medina were selective. They wanted a religion that fit their tribal pride. This is exactly what happened regarding the **File of Fatima**. People say: "We believe in the Prophet, but we refuse to talk about what happened to his daughter." They choose to "disbelieve" in the tragedy because it exposes the figures they have turned into idols. The Quran exposes this mindset as hypocrisy. If you truly believed in the Prophet Muhammad, you would condemn those who oppressed his only daughter.

We open this file because the culture of silence is a culture of participation. To register the crime of Fatima "against an unknown person" is to protect the criminals. The "File of Fatima" is the very scale of faith. It is the filter that separates those who seek the Reality of Religion from those who seek the Reality of the World. History is a living struggle. The oppression of Fatima is the root of every deviation that followed. We open the papers of this file to show the documents from the books of those who were pleased with the oppression. We will use their own ink to prove the crime, so that no one can say: "This is just a Shi'a myth."
Segment 4: The Inheritance of Concealment

Sheikh al-Ghizzi argues that the disease of concealing truth was inherited by the early Islamic establishment to protect the status of the first Caliphs. He asserts that the oppression of Lady Fatima was the first truth to be buried, as it challenged the legitimacy of post-prophetic leadership. He frames the file as the ultimate test of a believer's sincerity and their refusal to be "concurring partners" in injustice.

Why does the Quran insist on these stories? It is a mirror for us. The Prophet said: "You will follow the path of those before you, span by span and cubit by cubit." The disease of the Children of Israel—concealing the truth to protect their leaders—became the disease of this Ummah. When the truth of the house of Fatima was revealed, it threatened the new political structure. They began the process of **Concealment**. They used the excuse of "Unity" to bury the tragedy.

But I ask: Is the reputation of a fallible man more sacred than the broken rib of the Prophet’s daughter? This is where the File of Fatima becomes the dividing line. If you choose to hide her oppression, you are following the exact same path as those the Quran cursed. They say: "Opening this file creates fitna." I say: The concealment itself is the fitna! The root of every strife is the Saqifa and what was done to Fatima.

The establishment created a "sterilized history." They removed the papers that incriminated the founders. But the Imam of our Time is the "Avenger of Fatima." We open this file because the **Identity of the Shi'a** is built upon "Disowning" (Bara'a) the oppressors as much as "Loyalty" (Wilayah) to the Imams. In the next segments, we will go to the Sahihs of the Sunnis. We will show that even with their filter, the light of the oppression still shines through. The truth is a trust. We refuse to be concurring partners in the crime of silence.
Segment 5: Treachery and Lies in the Sahihs

Sheikh al-Ghizzi turns to Sahih Muslim and Sahih Bukhari. He highlights a narration where Umar ibn al-Khattab admits that Ali and Abbas viewed him and Abu Bakr as "liars, sinful, treacherous, and dishonest." He argues that if the Sunni world accepts these books as authentic, they must accept that this was the definitive stance of the Prophet's family toward the post-prophetic leadership.

Now we enter the Papers of Documentation. I will go to the heart of the "Reality of the World" that claims to represent the Sunnah. We go to Sahih Muslim, the Book of Jihad. There is a long Hadith regarding the dispute over Fadak. Umar ibn al-Khattab addresses Ali and Abbas. He says to Ali and Abbas:
You [Ali and Abbas] thought him [Abu Bakr] to be a liar, sinful, treacherous, and dishonest.
Then Umar continues:
Then Abu Bakr died and I became the successor... and you thought me [Umar] to be a liar, sinful, treacherous, and dishonest.

Listen carefully: **Liar, Sinful, Treacherous, and Dishonest**. These are the words Umar attributed to Imam Ali in the most authentic Sunni source! This document proves that Imam Ali never viewed their leadership as legitimate. The Tusi establishment tries to "interpret" it away, saying Ali was just angry. But the Imam is Infallible; his tongue only speaks the Truth.

This document is the Smoking Gun. If Ali viewed them as "treacherous and liars," how did they treat the daughter of the Prophet? They say the Sahaba were "all like stars." I ask: How can stars be "treacherous and liars" in the eyes of the Gateway to Knowledge? The File of Fatima begins with the realization that "Unity" is built on covering up the fact that the Prophet's family viewed the rulers as usurpers.
Segment 6: Manipulation in Sahih Bukhari

Sheikh al-Ghizzi demonstrates how Sahih Bukhari "censored" or "softened" narrations damaging to the first Caliphs. He shows that while Sahih Muslim explicitly lists Ali's condemnation, Bukhari replaces it with "you said such and such." He argues this systematic filtering is used to obscure the "Reality of Religion" and the truth of the Fatimid tragedy.

Let us look at the Art of Deception in the books of Hadith. Bukhari is the master of "Theological Filtering." In the same Hadith from Muslim—where Umar admits Ali viewed them as "liars, sinful, treacherous, and dishonest"—let's see what Bukhari does. Bukhari narrates the same scene, but writes:
And you [Ali and Abbas] thought him [Abu Bakr] to be so-and-so... and you thought me [Umar] to be so-and-so.

What is "so-and-so"? Why did Bukhari hide the words? Because "Liar" and "Treacherous" destroy the edifice of "Companionship." This is not "Scholarship"; this is **Forgery**. The Tusi establishment has inherited this same "Filtering Mindset." They hide Hadiths that are "too strong" because they want to maintain a peaceful relationship with the "Reality of the World." But the File of Fatima cannot be read through the filters of Bukhari. We need the Raw Truth.

If Imam Ali viewed them as "liars and treacherous," then the theft of Fadak was an act of treachery and the attack on the house was the act of the "sinful." We expose these techniques so the Shi'a youth can see how their history was stolen. They stole the history through the pen before they stole the rights through the sword. Reclaiming the file requires us to be "Intellectual Detectives."
Segment 7: Ibn Qutaiba’s Testimony of the Fire

Sheikh al-Ghizzi presents a document from the Sunni historian Ibn Qutaiba al-Dinawari. He highlights the narration where Umar gathered wood to burn the house because Imam Ali refused to pledge allegiance. He focuses on Umar's devastating response to being told Fatima was inside: "Even so!" (Wa in!). He argues this proves the threat was not a "Shi'a invention."

We move to a scholar the Sunnis call the "Trustworthy Historian": Ibn Qutaiba al-Dinawari. In his book "Al-Imama wa al-Siyasa", he records that Abu Bakr sent Umar to bring those gathered in the house. Ibn Qutaiba narrates that Umar came with fire and wood. He stood at the door and shouted:
By Him in whose hand is the soul of Umar, you shall come out to give the pledge, or I will burn the house over those who are in it!
The people were shocked and said:
O Umar, indeed Fatima is in the house!
And here is the word: Umar replied:
Even so! (Wa in!)

What does **"Even so"** mean? It means: "I do not care if she is the 'Leader of the Women of Paradise.' My desire for power is more important than the sanctity of her house." Ibn Qutaiba is not a Shi'a. If he records this, it is because the event was so well-documented it could not be erased.

They try to tell you today: "It was just a threat." I ask: Is the threat to burn the daughter of the Messenger not enough of a crime? The foundation of the Caliphate was built on the threat of fire. When you hear "Even so!", you realize the depth of the tragedy. It was a declaration of total war against the Household of the Prophet.
Segment 8: Al-Juwaini's Prophecy of the Broken Rib

Sheikh al-Ghizzi introduces the testimony of al-Juwaini from Fara'id al-Simtayn. He highlights a Prophetic prophecy where Muhammad foretells that Fatima will be "struck," her "rib broken," and her "unborn child miscarried." He argues this Sunni source corroborates the exact physical details found in Shi'a traditions.
We move to Muhaddith al-Juwaini, praised by al-Dhahabi. In his book "Fara'id al-Simtayn", he narrates that the Prophet looked at Fatima and wept. The Prophet said:
As if I see her... and humiliation has entered her home, her sanctity is violated, her right is usurped, her rib is broken, and her child is miscarried!
Listen to the Messenger! He used precise terms: **"Her rib is broken"** and **"Her child is miscarried."**

This is found in Sunni books. Why do they tell you these are Safavid "legends"? Here is a scholar centuries before the Safavids. The Prophet described her as: "Grieved, distressed, and oppressed." He prophesied her death as a "Martyr." This document proves the tragedy was a deliberate violation. The "Reality of the World" wanted its throne, even if it meant breaking the rib of the "Leader of the Women of Paradise." Even Bukhari could not hide that she remained angry. Al-Juwaini gives the reason: they broke into her home and struck her!
Segment 9: Umar’s Admission to Mu’awiyah

Sheikh al-Ghizzi references the "Letter of Umar to Mu’awiyah." He reads Umar’s own description of the attack, where he admits to striking Lady Fatima's hands, kicking the door, and pressing it against her until he heard her ribs breaking and the miscarriage occurred. The Sheikh argues this is a "confession from the perpetrator" that strips away all doubt.

We come now to an Admission. This is the Letter of Umar to Mu’awiyah, documented in Bihar al-Anwar. Umar recounts his "achievements." Umar describes the scene at the house. He says Fatima stood behind the door, trying to prevent entry. The letter states:
I struck her hands to make her release the door... then I kicked the door... I pressed the door against her with a powerful push, and I heard the sound of her ribs breaking!
He continues:
She cried out a cry that almost turned Medina upside down... then she fell, and the child in her womb was miscarried.

This is a First-Hand Report. The "Reality of the World" dismisses it as a "fabrication" because it is too honest. But why would a fabricator invent such graphic details that match the prophecies? The letter shows "Even so!" was not a bluff; it was followed by a Physical Assault. This is the evidence the "New Abbasids" fear. It reveals the "Reality of the Usurpation." The foundation of their politics was the blood of Mohsin and the broken bones of Zahra.
Segment 10: The Medical Cause of Death

Sheikh al-Ghizzi brings the testimony of Imam Ja'far al-Sadiq regarding the cause of Lady Fatima's death. He explains she died from physical trauma inflicted by Qunfudh under Umar's command. He concludes that her martyrdom is the "Mother of all Files" and the first step toward true connection with the Imam of the Time.
We reach the testimony of the Truthful One (al-Sadiq). In our authentic narrations, Imam al-Sadiq says explicitly:
The cause of her death was that Qunfudh, the servant of the man, struck her with the iron sheath of his sword by his command.
He continues:
...so she miscarried Mohsin, and she became severely ill from that [trauma].
This is the **Medical Verdict**. Fatima was a Martyr. She was murdered at eighteen years old.

The "Reality of the World" paints her death as "peaceful departure" from sadness. But the Imams tell of a Physical Assault. They tell of the "Deposit of Allah" spilled on the floor. Why insist on this? Because if she was struck, there are Oppressors. You cannot love the "Piece of the Prophet" and those who caused her death. This is the Logic of Sincerity. This is the foundation of the Social-Intellectual Current.
If you cannot face the blood on the door of Zahra, you will never have the courage to stand with the Mahdi. I leave you in the care of Fatima the oppressed, the martyr.
